title: eriosema rufescens
intro: Eriosema rufescens, also known as the red-barked eriosema, is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions of Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are derived from its roots and leaves. The health benefits of Eriosema rufescens include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and leaves are typically dried and crushed to make teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
